analogy: Think of "Disintegration of USSR" through the chapter's own opening images: O verview After the end of the bipolar structure of world politics in the early 1990s, it became clear that alternative centres of political and economic power could limit America’s dominance. Thus, in Europe, the European Union (EU) and, in Asia, the Association of South... The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
Disintegration of USSR is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that o verview After the end of the bipolar structure of world politics in the early 1990s, it became clear that alternative centres of political and economic power could limit America’s dominance.

The text explains that thus, in Europe, the European Union (EU) and, in Asia, the Association of South East Asian Nations (ASEAN), have emerged as forces to reckon with.

The text explains that while evolving regional solutions to their historical enmities and weaknesses, both the EU and the ASEAN have developed alternative institutions and conventions that build a more peaceful and cooperative regional order and have transformed the countries in the region into prosperous economies.

The text explains that the economic rise of China has made a dramatic impact on world politics.

The text explains that in this chapter, we take a look at some of these emerging alternative centres of power and assess their possible role in the future.

The text explains that chapter 2 Contemporary Centres of Power The two images here represent two phases of the history of China.

The text explains that the red poster – “The Socialist Road is the Broadest of All” – represents the ideology that guided China during its early phase after the Revolution.

The text explains that the photograph below is that of the city of Shanghai, the symbol of China’s new economic power.

The text explains that contemporary World Politics 16 E uropEan union As the Second World War came to an end, many of Europe’s leaders grappled with the ‘Question of Europe’.

The text explains that should Europe be allowed to revert to its old rivalries or be reconstructed on principles and institutions that would contribute to a positive conception of international relations?

The text explains that the Second World War shattered many of the assumptions and structures on which the European states had based their relations.

The text explains that in 1945, the European states confronted the ruin of their economies and the destruction of the assumptions and structures on which Europe had been founded.

The text explains that european integration after 1945 was aided by the Cold War.

The text explains that america extended massive financial help for reviving Europe’s economy under what was called the ‘Marshall Plan’.

The text explains that the US also created a new collective security structure under NATO.

The text explains that under the Marshall Plan, the Organisation for European Economic Cooperation (OEEC) was established in 1948 to channel aid to the west European states.

The text explains that source: http://europa.eu/abc/symbols/emblem/index_en.htm

The text explains that it is also the world’s second most important source of space and communications technology.

The text explains that 1979 June: First direct elections to the European Parliament 1981 January: Greece joins the EEC.

The text explains that at the end of the war, it confronted problems of nation- building, the ravages of poverty and economic backwardness and the pressure to align with one great power or another during the Cold War.

The text explains that source : www.aseansec.org Locate the ASEAN members on the map.

The text explains that source: http://www.unicef.org/eapro/EAP_map_final.gif Note: Maps on this site do not reflect a position by UNICEF on the legal status of any country or territory or the delimitation of any frontiers.

The text explains that the strength of its economy, together with other factors such as population, land mass, resources, regional location and political influence, adds to its power in significant ways.

The text explains that it had little choice but to fall back on its own resources and, for a brief period, on Soviet aid and advice.

The text explains that this model allowed China to use its resources to establish the foundations of an industrial economy on a scale that did not exist before.

The text explains that china has become the most important destination for foreign direct investment (FDI) anywhere in the world.

The text explains that japan has very few natural resources and imports most of its raw materials.

The text explains that the major factors responsible for its high human development include “successful land reforms, rural development, extensive human resources development and rapid equitable economic growth.” Other factors are export orientation, strong redistribution policies, public infrastructure development, effective institutions and governance.

The text explains that what are the components of the ASEAN Vision 2020?

The text explains that name the pillars and the objectives of the ASEAN Community.

The text explains that in what ways does the present Chinese economy differs from its command economy?

The text explains that how did the European countries resolve their post-Second World War problem?

The text explains that briefly outline the attempts that led to the formation of the European Union.

The text explains that what makes the European Union a highly influential regional organisation?

The text explains that the emerging economies of China and India have great potential to challenge the unipolar world.

The text explains that the Peace and prosperity of countries lay in the establishment and strengthening of regional economic organisations.

The text explains that identify the contentious issues between China and India.

The text explains that how could these be resolved for greater cooperation?

Source: NCERT Class 12 Contemporary World Politics, Chapter 2: Disintegration of USSR

prelims_traps: Do not treat 17 Contemporary C entres of Power and TIMELINE OF EUROPEAN as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Disintegration of USSR; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Disintegration of USSR through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use 17 Contemporary C entres of Power and TIMELINE OF EUROPEAN to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.
